title: rubia cordifolia f. pratensis
intro: Rubia cordifolia f. pratensis, also known as the Heart-leaved Madder, is a variant of the Rubia cordifolia plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its rich content of anthraquinone glycosides, which give it a long history of use in traditional medicine. The Heart-leaved Madder has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Rubia cordifolia f. pratensis include infusions, tinctures, and dried root extracts, which are often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
